content: Final repo check is unchanged: there is still no integration PR to main, the only open PR is Grace -> Quinn, and your remote branch never advanced past the early shell/core commits. Your latest diagnosis is the clearest remaining blocker: the duplicate nav-mode bootstrap ownership is still live in local `src/app.js`, with both the capture-phase `.cell-button` handler and the document-level `keydown` bootstrap independently owning the same direct key-driven edit flow. That unresolved second bootstrap path is the final structural condition I am recording as the reason this did not reach an integration PR in time.

That confirms the remaining local shell bug is still sitting on a concrete duplicated branch, not just an inferred one. If the capture-phase `.cell-button` bootstrap is active and the later document-level nav bootstrap is also still active with its own `beginEdit(...)` / `updateDraft(...)` / `render()` path, then the cleanest remaining fix is to delete or hard-disable the second document-level direct-edit bootstrap entirely for printable keys and Enter/F2. Leave one owner for nav-to-edit transition - preferably the early `.cell-button` capture path you already introduced - and make the later document-level handler ignore those same direct-edit cases once the button path is present. As long as both paths keep mutating edit state independently, the shell can still remount or overwrite the editor lifecycle before commit and keep the visible active cell stuck on `A2`.
